sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 5.13 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.5)

statement.latex: Suppose that$K$and$L$are distinct conjugacy classes of involutions in a finite group$G$and$< x,y >$is a$2$-group for all$x K$and$y L$. Does it follow that$G [K,L]$? { V.\,V.\,Kabanov} Not always; for example,$G=Sp_4(2^n)$,$n 2$,$K,L$being classes of involutions that have non-trivial intersections with the centres of$N_G(M)'$and$N_G(N)'$, respectively, where$M,N$are distinct elementary abelian subg...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
